Document:
[color=#897236][mono]──── [color=#FFA500][bold]IV. Arrest Procedure[/bold][/color] ───────────────────────────────[/mono][/color]
The following section outlines the proper procedures for handling a lawful arrest.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]1. Move in to arrest the suspect[/color][/head]
- State your reason for the arrest and the charges being pressed against the suspect. If the suspect is inside of a shuttle, Enforcers should use shortband or holopads to contact them. - If the suspect is not compliant, non-lethal force such as a disabler may used to subdue the suspect. - If the suspect is non-compliant and armed, flees, or is an immediate threat to others or the arresting Enforcer, lethal force may be used to subdue the suspect. - Handcuff the suspect and prepare to transport them to the TDF Auric Outpost for processing. - Suspects should not be left at a scene if they are dead or incapacitated. Enforcers should properly secure the suspect.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]2. Store the suspect's personal effects[/color][/head]
Enforcers responsible for the arrest shall remove the suspect's equipment and store all personal effects in an evidence locker. Any contraband should be seized and exchanged for TCC if possible. If necessary, Enforcers should check the prisoner for implants using empty implanters.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]3. Brig and sentence the suspect[/color][/head]
The suspect shall then be placed in a cell and issued a prisoner jumpsuit and prisoner headset. The suspect is now a prisoner. Once the prisoner has been secured in their cell, they must be informed of the crimes they have been charged with and the length of their sentence. If available, the Warden should be the one to sentence the prisoner. If the Warden is unavailable, the Commander should be called to sentence the prisoner. If the Commander is unavailable, an Enforcer unaffiliated with the arrest should be called to sentence the prisoner. If there is no Warden, Commander, or unaffiliated Enforcer available in a timely manner, the arresting Enforcer may sentence the prisoner.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]4. Impounding the prisoner's ship[/color][/head]
Enforcers handling the arrest shall transport the prisoner's vessel to the TDF outpost, where it will remain docked for the duration of the sentence. The vessel shall be searched for contraband, and any illegal items found shall be confiscated. For ships acquired through naval engagement, the vessel's weapons shall be removed before the ship is returned to its owner upon completion of the sentence. If the ship were to suffer from damages while in transit or in TDF custody, the Enforcer responsible for impounding the vessel will be held accountable, and liable to pay for any damages suffered to the prisoner's vessel. If the damages in question were due to the prisoner's actions or from an active combat engagement, the Enforcer responsible for the impoundment will not be held accountable for the damages.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]5. Prisoner treatment[/color][/head]
Prisoners have certain rights that must be upheld by security and TDF personnel. Prisoners MUST be provided the following: - Adequate Medical Care, with moral, spiritual, or legal counseling if it is requested and available. A prisoner must receive a medical check to ensure they are uninjured before they are submitted to confinement. Medication must be of a reasonable level of skillful, quality care. Prisoners are expected to be provided privacy when receiving medical treatment. - Prisoners clothing must always be made available for prisoners to change into. Confiscated clothing should be returned upon request provided it is not evidence or provides a clear risk to other prisoners or Enforcer's safety. - Food and water for organic prisoners must be available and provided on request and power for silicon prisoners must be provided. Prisoner possessions are to be stored until the end of the sentence. All items that are not evidence in the commission of a crime, or contraband are to be returned upon the end of the prisoner's sentence. - Should the holding cells, or perma cell become uninhabitable, prisoners must be securely relocated to another area until their confinement area is made habitable. Prisoners attempting to escape confinement when the confinement area is seen as being unsafe/dangerous are not to be charged for any actions they took during that process.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]6. Prisoner release[/color][/head]
Upon completion of the sentence, TDF personnel shall release the prisoner. The prisoner's personal effects and vessel shall be returned without obstruction or unnecessary conflict.
NEXT PAGE --> [textlink="V. Chain of Command" link="SOPChainOfCommand"]
